title: digitaria ciliaris subsp. marginata
intro: Digitaria ciliaris subsp. marginata, commonly known as finger grass, is a subspecies of grass in the genus Digitaria. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of finger grass include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Finger grass can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
